maintenance.after_every_run: Update the selected item's durable status and evidence; do not reprioritize unrelated items mechanically.
maintenance.weekly: Re-score ready and planned items from fresh GSC, public health, experiment, product, and authority evidence.
maintenance.monthly_or_strategy_event: Ask the user to confirm material destination expansion, monetization direction, risk changes, or site continuation decisions.

goals: Validate relevant organic demand before scaling destination count.
Finish San Diego as the first deep, differentiated family-planning cluster.
Create a repeatable loop from evidence to one reviewed experiment to observation to keep/iterate/stop.
Build useful tools and source-worthy assets rather than generic travel prose.

items.title: Reframe the Las Vegas stay page as a constraint-led verification guide
items.production_invariant: The affected URL remains canonical and indexable, contains exact total-fee, room-assignment, pool-access, smoke/noise-exposure, and route-friction checks, and contains no unreviewed best-area or best-fit verdict presented as fact.
items.success_signal: The existing page provides useful constraint-led booking checks without firm human-gated area, hotel-fit, smoke/noise, route, pool, or family-suitability claims; focused/native QA and independent review pass.
items.gsc_evidence_classification: fresh authenticated read-only API snapshot collected 2026-07-15 with finalized performance data through 2026-07-13; finalized-conservative completeness based on a two-day lag

desktop, 390px, and 320px browser checks passed without page overflow or implementation console errors
canonical, indexability, and sitemap membership remained unchanged

items.result: Reframed the existing Las Vegas stay page as a constraint-led verification guide with explicit total-fee, room-assignment, pool-access, smoke/noise-exposure, route-friction, and current-policy checks. Firm area and property-fit verdicts were replaced by hypotheses, UNKNOWN states, exact checks, and human-review questions.
items.notes: This is an existing unprotected URL. Do not rank hotels, add a new page, alter sitemap/indexability, or touch any protected target. Review cycle 1 passed.

items.title: Build an unpublished San Diego nap-and-pacing itinerary prototype
items.success_signal: A locally testable, unpublished prototype converts child age, nap/reset needs, trip length, weather, and transport constraints into a transparent pacing plan without publishing route, safety, or suitability claims as verified fact.
items.gsc_evidence_classification: reused authenticated UI import collected 2026-07-13 with data through 2026-07-12; directional, not fresh API evidence; GitHub workflow 29349159935 skipped collection because GSC_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON is not configured

production SEO QA passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings

items.result: Built a deterministic local planner that converts age, trip length, reset, weather, transport, pace, stroller, and sensory constraints into disclosed day shapes, triggered rules, and current verification checks.
items.notes: Keep the prototype outside site/ until a later reviewed integration action is separately selected. Every output is model-derived, needs human review, and avoids exact route/timing, safety, access, or suitability claims.

items.title: Audit official-source coverage and reuse terms for the San Diego Family Reset Atlas
items.success_signal: A public-safe matrix establishes source availability, freshness, reuse terms, attribution needs, and human-review gaps for all 13 enumerated candidate anchors without publishing claims or contacting an external party.
items.gsc_evidence_classification: fresh authenticated read-only API snapshot collected 2026-07-16 with finalized performance data through 2026-07-14; finalized-conservative completeness based on a two-day lag

items.result: Audited all 13 candidate anchors against official first-party or government sources. All 13 meet the source-availability threshold, but structured reuse remains UNKNOWN for ordinary webpage facts; City Open Data may support location geometry for two public-park anchors under its terms. Real records and every firm trust-sensitive claim remain blocked, while an unpublished synthetic-fixture prototype may proceed.
items.notes: Research only. Independent review cycle 2 returned PASS after closing both cycle-1 P2 findings; no P0-P3 findings remain. No permission request, outreach, source-prose republication, real-record ingestion, site edit, or external-account action occurred.

items.title: Evaluate the deployed San Diego stay-checklist experiment
items.success_signal: Fresh query/page and indexing evidence is compared with the July 13 baseline and results in a keep, iterate, or stop decision without overstating causality.

items.title: Create the San Diego family-hotel research engine evidence pack
items.success_signal: An internal evidence pack defines San Diego family hotel candidates, family decision criteria, official source facts, booking checks, review/community-signal themes, conflicts, unknowns, and publication-ready claim labels without publishing a new hotel page.
items.evidence: Fresh authenticated read-only API snapshot collected 2026-07-18 with finalized data through 2026-07-16; healthy housekeeping did not preempt. The internal three-property pack records current official facts, attempted booking checks, bounded review/community themes, conflicts, unknowns, claim labels, and release gates.
items.qa: 15/15 GSC schema/privacy tests; six snapshot validations; native and production SEO QA 0 errors/0 warnings; 19 source rows; cached and new-file whitespace checks passed.
items.notes: User approved replacing the blanket hotel human-review blocker with a high-quality research workflow on 2026-07-18. This action is research-only: do not publish a standalone hotel page, add hotel affiliate CTAs, copy review text, scrape prohibited sources, request indexing, or make safety/firsthand/family-suitability claims as verified.

items.title: Design the clean San Diego family-hotel page brief
items.success_signal: A concise page brief defines the uncluttered San Diego family-hotel page structure, candidate breadth, category logic, comparison fields, evidence modules, price-band standard, review-signal rules, and publication gates before any live page is built.
items.evidence: Manual user instruction clarified that the next San Diego hotel step should broaden beyond three hotels, use approximate room-per-night bands rather than exact booking-flow totals, treat crib/rollaway/connecting-room facts as useful but not mandatory, and prioritize recent family/kids review signals.
items.review: Cycle 2 PASS after closing price-band durability, stale booking-gate, and exact-path P2 findings.
items.qa: jq empty ops/seo-roadmap.json; node tools/seo-qa.mjs 0 errors/0 warnings; git diff --check passed.
items.notes: Page brief and expanded candidate planning only. Do not publish a page, add affiliate CTAs, request indexing, deploy, or create ordinal hotel rankings in this planning action. Price bands in the candidate brief remain provisional hypotheses until FT-RES-005 creates per-hotel source rows.

items.title: Expand the San Diego family-hotel evidence pack to 6-8 candidates
items.success_signal: The San Diego evidence pack covers 6-8 primary hotels with official facts, approximate nightly room bands, parking/fee signals, family-relevant recent review themes, community-signal separation, conflicts, and parent-facing verification notes without publishing a page.
items.notes: Do not require exact booking-screen totals for this pass. Use sourced approximate price bands and keep final all-in totals, exact room availability, cancellation rules, crib/rollaway/connecting-room guarantees, safety, quiet-room, stroller, and firm family-suitability claims as verify-before-booking or human-review-gated.
